memoryArtifacts.headline: First Bagley on the Planet
memoryArtifacts.description:
THE FIRST BAGLEY

Richard de Baguley was born Richard de Massey. He changed his name to Richard de Baguley when he inherited Baggaleigh from his father John de Massey.

zh.Knutsford, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾可努恰福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley於1196年出生在英國英格蘭爾巴古利時，他的父親John de Baguley24歲，母親Ellen de Huyton18歲。他於大約1230年與Alice de Vernon在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1263年在他的家鄉享年68歲。

zh-hans.Knutsford, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰爾可努恰福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley于1196年出生在英国英格兰爾巴古利时，他的父亲John de Baguley24岁，母亲Ellen de Huyton18岁。他于大约1230年与Alice de Vernon在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1263年在他的家乡享年68岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Baguley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley (Cheshire). The placename is from Old English bagga denoting an unidentified animal, perhaps the badger, + lēah ‘wood, clearing’. Compare Bagley .

variants.name: Bagley
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley in Cheshire or from any of several places called Bagley, in Devon, Gloucestershire, Shropshire, Somerset, and Yorkshire. These get their names either from the Old English personal name Bacga + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ or from an unattested Old English word, bagga, for a ‘bag-shaped’ object or creature + lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Baguley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley (Cheshire). The placename is from Old English bagga denoting an unidentified animal, perhaps the badger, + lēah ‘wood, clearing’. Compare Bagley .

variants.name: Bagley
meanings: English: habitational name from Baguley in Cheshire or from any of several places called Bagley, in Devon, Gloucestershire, Shropshire, Somerset, and Yorkshire. These get their names either from the Old English personal name Bacga + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ or from an unattested Old English word, bagga, for a ‘bag-shaped’ object or creature + lēah.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley was born in 1196, in Baguley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Baguley, was 24 and his mother, Ellen de Huyton, was 18. He married Alice de Vernon about 1230, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died about 1263, in his hometown, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley est né en 1196, à Baguley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Baguley, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Ellen de Huyton, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Alice de Vernon environ 1230, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé environ 1263, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley 1196 in Baguley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Baguley 24 und seine Mutter Ellen de Huyton 18. Er hat um 1230 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Alice de Vernon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb um 1263 im Alter von 68 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley è nato il nel 1196 nel Baguley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Baguley, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Ellen de Huyton, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Alice de Vernon circa 1230, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì circa 1263, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 68 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguleyが1196年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアバグリーで生まれた時，父John de Baguleyは24歳，母Ellen de Huytonは18歳であった。約1230年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアでAlice de Vernonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は約1263年に故郷で68歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley(이)가 1196년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 바구리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Baguley(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen de Huyton(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1230년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Alice de Vernon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1263년 그의 고향에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley nasceu em 1196, em Baguley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Baguley, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Ellen de Huyton, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice de Vernon aproximadamente 1230, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1263, em sua cidade natal, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard de Baguley - Lord of Baguley родился в 1196 г. в местности багули, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Baguley было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Ellen de Huyton, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice de Vernon около 1230 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер около 1263 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 68 лет.

Se casó con Alice de Vernon aproximadamente 1230, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1263, en su pueblo, a la edad de 68 años.
